parsed_review.summary: CP00-180 (Matter Core Risk A, RP05.P02.M09.S03-S12, 10 units) binds a descriptor-only sensitive-service precheck boundary over the CP00-178 service entrypoint, and does NOT over-claim. manifest.production_ready=false, construction-inspection verdict=PENDING/production_ready=false, adjudication "Production ready after adjudication: no", and command-evidence status=in_progress are mutually consistent for a position before the pack-level Claude review is captured. Implementation matches contract/baseline: deliverable distribution implementation:6/security_audit:2/ui:2 and RP05.P02.M09=10 are enforced by validateMatterCoreCp180Coverage; the no-write/no-real-data boundary holds (service.js freezeServiceResult stamps writes_product_state, evaluates_runtime_permission, writes_audit_event, creates/updates/deletes_database_rows, acquires_runtime_lock, dispatches_review/approval_route, executes_api_handler, issues_network_request, implements_loop_engine = false; lock_status "not_acquired_descriptor_only"; persistence_boundary "never_write_descriptor_only"); sensitive prechecks fail closed to customer-safe error codes (e.g. tenant_boundary_mismatch->MATTER_CORE_TENANT_BOUNDARY_MISMATCH, missing_matter_trace->MATTER_CORE_MATTER_TRACE_REQUIRED, idempotency_key_required->MATTER_CORE_IDEMPOTENCY_REQUIRED). Authority boundary is preserved at every layer: contract.sensitive_service_precheck_boundary and contract.no_write_attestation plus registry MATTER_CORE_CP180_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION set claude_is_final_approval / promotes_claude_to_final_approval = false and enterprise_trust_claim_from_local_validation / claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation = false; validateMatterCoreCp180SensitiveBoundary asserts these, and the run is read-only (Read/Grep/Glob). CP178+ review hardening is intact: readiness auth_preflight passed (exit_code 0, 1434-byte valid-JSON stdout, review_attempt_counted=false), so this is not an auth-failure / 0-byte / malformed-JSON / fenced-JSON / tool-call-shaped attempt, and forbidden_review_evidence (auth_failure, stdout_0_bytes, malformed_json, fenced_json_plus_prose, tool_call_shaped_output, ...) is codified in registry/contract and reused by the CP180 Claude packet. Hermes patterns are confined to deterministic control-plane packet/evidence generation (createMatterCoreCp180HermesEvidencePacket; H05 command-evidence with spec_requirement_validate + rp00_control_plane_contract_validate), not product runtime. No P0/P1 defects found. Three P3 advisories cover expected mid-pipeline bookkeeping (downstream construction-inspection/adjudication/command-evidence pending with empty command sets; empty claude-review-result ledger) and one functionally-harmless carryover (generic review_status-vs-lifecycle coupling in validators.js, also noted in the CP00-179 review). None block pack or goal closeout; final enterprise-trust acceptance remains a human decision and is not asserted by this read-only review.

findings.title: Downstream construction-inspection, adjudication, and command-evidence are staged-pending with empty command sets
findings.details: construction-inspection.json has verdict=PENDING with tests_and_validation.commands=[] and all_passed=false (h05_gate_passed=false, c05_gate_passed=false); adjudication.md records all P0-P3 findings as pending and 'Production ready after adjudication: no'; command-evidence.json has status=in_progress with commands=[] and hermes_gate H05 status=pending. The local/Hermes validation command results (the 11 validation_commands) are not yet captured into pack artifacts. This is the expected state for a pack positioned before its pack-level Claude review is normalized, and it is honestly reflected (manifest.production_ready=false) rather than over-claimed, so it is advisory rather than a defect.
findings.recommendation: Before commit, complete the hardened pipeline (normalize -> receipt_validate -> adjudication -> construction_inspection -> final_closeout_validation) so command-evidence.commands, construction-inspection.tests_and_validation.commands, and the H05/C05 gate booleans are populated from real local runs, and only then resolve production_ready.
findings.post_review_disposition: advisory_open — non-blocking; expected mid-pipeline state to be completed by downstream hardened-review steps before commit

findings.title: claude-review-result ledger arrays are empty and exactly_one_valid_pack_level_claude_review_run is false
findings.details: valid_review_receipts=[] and invalid_review_attempts=[] with exactly_one_valid_pack_level_claude_review_run=false and status=review_pending; no raw-output.json or review-receipt.json exists yet for CP00-180. This is accurate for a review that has not yet been normalized into a receipt: the readiness auth_preflight passed and review_attempt_counted=false, so there is also no invalid attempt to log. Non-blocking bookkeeping.
findings.recommendation: After this read-only review is captured and validated by the hardened pipeline, append the resulting valid receipt to valid_review_receipts, set exactly_one_valid_pack_level_claude_review_run=true, and confirm review-receipt.json/raw-output.json refs resolve before closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: advisory_open — non-blocking; bookkeeping to be populated when this review receipt is normalized

findings.title: validateMatterCoreRecord validates review_status against lifecycle_statuses for all model types
findings.details: record.review_status is validated against definition.lifecycle_statuses generically for every model type. Only MatterWikiSection (whose lifecycle_statuses are the review vocabulary) and the graph node/edge models carry a meaningful review_status; for other models review_status is absent, so the guarded check (record?.review_status && ...) is a harmless no-op. The behavior is functionally correct and fail-closed, but it conflates lifecycle and review-status vocabularies. This is a carryover of the CP00-179 review observation (CP179-R03) and validators.js is an in-scope CP00-180 implementation ref.
findings.recommendation: Optionally add a per-model review_status_vocabulary (or scope the review_status check to models that declare one) so lifecycle state and review state are not validated against the same list. No behavior change is required for CP00-180 closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: advisory_open — non-blocking carryover from CP00-179; no functional impact
